charter: M19: implement a repo-local capability documentation generator for tusq.dev. Add a tusq docs command that reads tusq.manifest.json and emits Markdown review/adoption documentation for capabilities, including approval state, side_effect_class, sensitivity_class, auth_hints, provenance, examples, constraints, redaction, and manifest version metadata. Keep it local/offline and do not add live execution semantics.
acceptance_contract: CLI help lists tusq docs with usage
default output is a deterministic Markdown capability document
Markdown includes manifest metadata and per-capability governance/provenance sections
npm test covers docs generation and existing smoke/eval suites remain green
README and website CLI docs mention tusq docs honestly as generated documentation not hosted cloud
